prompt: I'm stuck between a single orchestrator, a state-machine-driven workflow, and a set of thin skills coordinated by handoffs for a new repository-maintenance feature. I haven't decided whether resumability or simplicity matters more yet. Help me figure out the architecture options before we commit to implementation.
assumptions: No existing codebase or stack constraints were provided, so the design was treated as greenfield.
The repository-maintenance feature may include external side effects such as inspection, file edits, issue/PR creation, CI waits, and human approval.
The main unresolved trade-off is between coarse step-boundary resumability and full workflow-state durability/auditability.
Thin skills can be useful execution modules even if they are not chosen as the primary control-plane architecture.
